skill.name: phylogenetic-tree-styler
skill.description: Style phylogenetic tree visualizations from Newick format files with taxonomy color blocks, bootstrap values, and timeline annotations for publication-ready scientific figures.
skill.summary: Transform Newick phylogenetic trees into styled output images with taxonomy color blocks, bootstrap values, and timelines.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 78 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly flagged markdown code blocks as shell backtick execution, matplotlib hex color codes as cryptographic algorithms, security checklist items as path traversal, and sample CSV data as memory-mapped file access. The skill is a legitimate bioinformatics visualization tool that reads Newick tree files and taxonomy CSV data then renders styled output images. No malicious intent detected. External command usage (python/pip) and filesystem access (read input, write output) are legitimate and well-documented.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks flagged as shell backtick execution
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 56 instances of markdown backtick formatting as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are documentation code blocks showing example commands (pip install, python execution) in SKILL.md and references/runtime_checklist.md. Markdown backticks are formatting syntax, not executable shell code.

low_findings.title: Matplotlib hex colors flagged as weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged hex color code strings in scripts/main.py as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. Lines 23-26 contain standard matplotlib hex color values (e.g., '#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e') used for taxonomy color block visualization. These are color codes, not cryptographic material.

low_findings.title: Matplotlib hex colors flagged as C2 keywords
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged hex color codes as C2 (Command and Control) keywords at scripts/main.py:23. The hex strings are standard matplotlib color palette values used for rendering taxonomy color blocks on phylogenetic tree visualizations. No C2 infrastructure present.

low_findings.title: Security checklist items flagged as path traversal
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged SKILL.md lines 182 and 185 as path traversal. These lines are items in a security checklist that describe what the skill prevents: 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)'. This is documentation describing security measures, not actual path traversal usage.

low_findings.title: Sample CSV data flagged as memory-mapped file access
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged example/taxonomy.csv:2 and SKILL.md:155 as memory-mapped file access. The taxonomy.csv file contains sample species classification data (name, domain, phylum, class). SKILL.md:155 shows an example CSV data row. Both are sample data, not memory mapping operations.

low_findings.title: Audit result JSON flagged as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged phylogenetic-tree-styler_audit_result_v2.json:125,195 and SKILL.md:64,76,205 as system reconnaissance. The JSON file contains pre-computed skill evaluation scores and test results. The SKILL.md lines contain workflow documentation about output discipline and validation commands. None of these perform system reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Source repository URL flagged as hardcoded network URL
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged SKILL.md:7 as a hardcoded network URL. This line contains a markdown link to the GitHub source repository (https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). This is documentation referencing the skill's source location, not executable network code.

content.user_title: Style Phylogenetic Trees with Taxonomy Colors and Bootstrap Values
content.value_statement: Researchers struggle to create publication-ready phylogenetic tree visualizations with taxonomy annotations. This skill transforms Newick format trees into styled output images with color-coded taxonomy blocks, bootstrap support values, and optional evolutionary timelines.
actual_capabilities: Reads Newick format phylogenetic tree files and renders them as styled PNG, PDF, or SVG images
Adds taxonomy color blocks to leaf nodes based on domain, phylum, and class classification data from CSV files
Displays bootstrap support values on internal nodes with color-coded confidence thresholds
Creates evolutionary timelines with configurable root age and time scale annotations
Supports customization of branch colors, leaf label colors, image dimensions, and output DPI
Processes species taxonomy information from CSV files to generate color-coded classification annotations
limitations: Only supports Newick format tree files as input; other formats are not supported
Cannot perform phylogenetic inference or tree construction from sequence data
Taxonomy color blocks are limited to domain, phylum, and class hierarchy levels
Requires ete3 library which has complex dependency requirements on some systems

use_cases.title: Publication-Ready Tree Figures
use_cases.description: A researcher has a phylogenetic tree in Newick format and needs a styled figure for a journal publication with bootstrap values and color-coded taxonomy groups.

use_cases.title: Teaching Phylogenetics Concepts
use_cases.description: An instructor wants to create clear visual examples of evolutionary relationships for classroom materials with distinct color coding for different taxonomic groups.

use_cases.title: Automated Pipeline Visualization
use_cases.description: A bioinformatician needs to add automated phylogenetic tree visualization to their data analysis pipeline with consistent styling and bootstrap value display.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Tree Styling
prompt_templates.prompt: Style the phylogenetic tree in tree.nwk and save the output as tree_styled.png using default settings.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a Newick tree file and wants a quick styled visualization.

prompt_templates.title: Tree with Bootstrap Values
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a styled phylogenetic tree from tree.nwk showing bootstrap support values above 70. Save the output as tree_bootstrap.png.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to display confidence values on tree branch splits.

prompt_templates.title: Taxonomy Color-Coded Tree
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a phylogenetic tree visualization from tree.nwk with taxonomy color blocks using taxonomy.csv for species classification data. Color by domain level. Save as tree_taxonomy.png.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has taxonomy data and wants color-coded species group annotations.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Publication-Ready Tree
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive phylogenetic tree visualization from tree.nwk with the following features: show bootstrap values above 70, add taxonomy color blocks from taxonomy.csv, display a timeline with root age of 500 Mya, set branch color to dark gray, and output as PDF at 300 DPI saved as tree_full.pdf.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a publication-ready figure with all available visual features.

output_examples.input: Style the tree in tree.nwk with taxonomy colors and bootstrap values.
output_examples.output: A styled PNG image of a phylogenetic tree with colored branches, taxonomy color blocks at leaf nodes colored by domain, and bootstrap support values displayed at internal nodes with blue color intensity based on confidence level.

output_examples.input: Generate a comprehensive tree visualization as a PDF with timeline.
output_examples.output: A publication-ready PDF vector graphic of an evolutionary tree showing a timeline scale from 500 Mya to present, domain-specific color blocks at leaf nodes, high-confidence bootstrap values in bold dark blue, and a legend explaining the color coding scheme.

best_practices: Always validate the Newick file format before running the styling script to avoid parse errors
Use bootstrap threshold of 70 or higher to only display statistically significant support values
Provide a complete taxonomy CSV with consistent species names matching the tree leaf labels
anti_patterns: Using unsupported tree file formats like FASTA or Nexus will cause the script to fail
Requesting tree construction from sequence data goes beyond this skill documented scope
Omitting the taxonomy file when requesting taxonomy color blocks will produce a tree without color annotations

faq.question: What input formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill only supports the Newick format (.nwk or .newick). Other formats like FASTA or Nexus are not supported.

faq.question: Can I create a tree from my DNA sequences?
faq.answer: No. This skill styles existing trees and does not perform phylogenetic inference or tree construction from sequence data.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: The skill can produce PNG, PDF, and SVG image formats using the --format parameter.

faq.question: How do I add taxonomy colors to the tree?
faq.answer: Provide a CSV file with columns for species name, domain, phylum, and class using the --taxonomy-file parameter and the colors will be applied automatically.

faq.question: What does the bootstrap value mean?
faq.answer: Bootstrap values indicate confidence in each branch split. Values above 70 are moderately supported and values above 90 are strongly supported.

faq.question: Can I customize the colors used for taxonomy blocks?
faq.answer: Color schemes are predefined in the TAXONOMY_COLORS dictionary inside scripts/main.py. You can modify these values to customize the color palette.
